data.title: Rocket Pool to Hold Live Stream on YouTube on February 18
data.description:
Rocket Pool will host a live stream of Saturn One launch on YouTube on February 18 at 00:00 UTC.

RPL Info

Rocket Pool (RPL) is a decentralized staking protocol designed for Ethereum 2.0. Unlike traditional staking pools, Rocket Pool aims to create a more decentralized and resilient staking infrastructure for Ethereum.

Ethereum 2.0 requires 32 ETH to run a validator. Rocket Pool allows users to stake with significantly smaller amounts by pooling resources from various participants. Rocket Pool categorizes its participants into two groups: regular users and “nodes”. While regular users can delegate their ETH without the hassle of managing infrastructure, nodes take on the responsibility of ensuring the validators run smoothly. Users who delegate their ETH to Rocket Pool receive rETH tokens in return, which represent their stake in the pool and can be freely traded on external markets.

RPL is a native token of Rocket Pool that is used as a collateral mechanism for nodes and acts to incentivize the proper operation of the network. Nodes are required to hold RPL as collateral to ensure their honest operation and to mitigate potential risks to delegating users.

data.title: iExec RLC to Hold Live Stream on February 17
data.description:
iExec RLC announced a live discussion featuring ShadowSwap, one of the Hack4Privacy winners, focused on the development of a confidential decentralized exchange. The conversation, scheduled for February 17 at 5 PM UTC, will examine the technology behind private trading infrastructure and outline the project’s roadmap.

RLC Info

iExec is a decentralized cloud computing platform based on the Ethereum blockchain. It provides on-demand computing resources for decentralized applications (dApps) on its cloud. Unlike traditional cloud services, iExec offers a blockchain network that integrates various computing resources, facilitating the smooth operation of dApps by distributing their computational processes.

iExec ensures efficient dApp functioning by offloading processing-intensive computations off-chain, maintaining the uninterrupted operation of on-chain blockchain activities. This decentralization of computations is achieved using XtremWeb-HEP, an open-source Desktop Grid Software. This software facilitates Desktop Grid computing, which leverages unused computing power from various sources for dApp processing. With XtremWeb-HEP, iExec can provide a vast array of essential features, like fault-tolerance, multi-application support, and hybrid public/private infrastructure, allowing dApps to access any computing resource within the iExec framework.

The RLC token is iExec’s native cryptocurrency. It serves as the primary medium to access the services within the iExec infrastructure. This means that to utilize iExec’s decentralized computing resources or other platform-related services, users would primarily engage through the RLC token.

data.title: Trust Wallet Launches Flap Bonding Curve Trading
data.description:
Trust Wallet has launched Flap bonding curve token trading within its Meme Rush section. The feature provides fast execution, a simplified interface, and early-stage price discovery for newly issued tokens.

TWT Info

Trust Wallet is a multichain mobile application designed to provide users with seamless access to various blockchains and their associated transactions and Decentralized Applications (DApps). Unlike its browser-based counterparts, Trust Wallet facilitates instant accessibility to multiple blockchains upon installation, eliminating the need for manual blockchain-specific configurations.

Upon installation, users are granted full control over their seed phrase, underlining the non-custodial nature of Trust Wallet. This design prioritizes user security and asset ownership. Within the wallet, users can store and visualize NFTs from both Ethereum and BNB Smart Chain. In addition to its storage capacities, Trust Wallet also incorporates an integrated DApp browser, enhancing its functionality. Further diversifying its features, Trust Wallet allows for in-app staking, asset swapping across-chains facilitated by platforms like Binance DEX and 1inch, and direct cryptocurrency purchases using various fiat on-ramps.

Functioning as a governance token within the ecosystem, the Trust Wallet Token (TWT) empowers its holders with decision-making capabilities regarding the app’s developmental trajectory. Through TWT, users can both propose and vote on pivotal updates, new features, and essential changes, reinforcing a community-driven approach to Trust Wallet’s evolution.

data.title: Cronos Achieves ISO/IEC 42001:2023 Certification
data.description:
Crypto.com has announced that it has obtained the ISO/IEC 42001:2023 certification, an international standard for AI management systems. The company becomes the first digital asset platform to receive this certification.

CRO Info

Cronos is an open source decentralized blockchain developed by Crypto.com, a payment, trading and financial services company.

It aims to massively scale the Web3 user community by providing builders with the ability to instantly port apps and crypto assets from other chains with low cost, high throughput, and fast finality. Cronos utilizes Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) technology, which allows it to be compatible with existing decentralized applications (dApps) built on Ethereum.

CRO is a native platform token that can be used to conduct transactions and interact with decentralized applications (dApps).

data.title: Anderson Cooper to leave CBS' '60 Minutes' program, source says
data.description:
Anderson Cooper will leave CBS News' "60 Minutes" program after nearly two decades, a source familiar with the matter said on Monday, in the latest staffing shake-up to hit the storied news magazine amid broader newsroom changes.

Cooper has been a "60 Minutes" correspondent through a deal between CBS News and CNN since the 2006-2007 season, according to his page on the CBS News website.

His recent work for the program has included reports on so-called COVID-19 long-haulers and a wreck found near Mobile, Alabama, that is believed to be the last slave ship to have landed in the U.S.

Cooper joined Warner Bros Discovery's television network CNN in 2001 and has reported on the Iraq War, Hurricane Katrina and the Gulf of Mexico oil spill.

Lachlan Cartwright's Breaker newsletter first reported Cooper's impending departure from "60 Minutes."

data.title: GWA Loses Bull as Headwinds Strengthen — Market Talk
data.description: Headwinds buffeting GWA's growth outlook are too strong for Macquarie. It downgrades GWA to neutral, from outperform, and lowers its price target by 8.6% to A$2.65/share following the building fixtures supplier's 1H result. "End markets continue to show weakness, which coupled with monetary policy changes, may be an ongoing theme," says Macquarie. It also points to concerns over consumer confidence and increased spending on product development. Together, these could weigh on growth in the near term. "An attractive 6% dividend yield and 9% free cash flow yield provide a strong underpin," says Macquarie. "But softer growth and a shifting macro context counter our near-term enthusiasm in the thesis." GWA ended Monday at A$2.62. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.description:
KC platform launch drove 49.5% SaaS revenue growth and 106.9% NPAT increase year-over-year, expanding the addressable market 4-5x. AI is fully embedded, with strong cash flow and a validated pipeline in both SMB and large segments.
